summary: Iteration 30: Backup/Restore/Factory-Reset + Emergent cleanup + Seed data verification. Backend: 14/14 pytest passed. Frontend: BackupAdmin page renders all 13 data-testid elements correctly (stats: 193 docs, 15 collections, 51 files / 14.59 MB), Download DB / Download Uploads / Restore inputs+buttons / Factory Reset reveal + confirm-input + gated Reset button all working. 0 console errors, 0 network failures on /admin/backup. Public / does not contain 'emergent-badge' or 'Made with Emergent'. requirements.txt does not contain emergentintegrations. index.html has no emergent scripts or posthog. Startup code does not seed executive_committee or members demo data.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py now ~4610 lines - still well beyond 700-line guideline (see iter29 comment). Backup/restore/factory-reset section should ideally live in its own /app/backend/routers/admin_backup.py

Factory reset & restore endpoints wrap DB operations in try/except but do not use a MongoDB transaction; a partial failure mid-collection could leave DB in inconsistent state. Acceptable for admin-only destructive ops but worth noting.

admin_factory_reset accepts a raw dict body instead of a Pydantic model — inconsistent with rest of codebase (consider FactoryResetRequest model with confirm/keep_admin/clear_uploads fields for OpenAPI docs).

admin_restore_uploads writes any path from ZIP directly under UPLOAD_DIR (zip-slip risk if a malicious ZIP contains '../' entries). Consider `os.path.commonpath([target.resolve(), UPLOAD_DIR.resolve()]) == str(UPLOAD_DIR.resolve())` before writing.

tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile is created with delete=False for backup ZIPs but the temp file is never cleaned up after FileResponse streams. Over time /tmp will accumulate ZIPs. Consider BackgroundTask cleanup.
Uploads restore skips filenames starting with '_' - correct for _backup_info.json, but would also skip any legitimate file starting with underscore. Better: exact-match on '_backup_info.json'.
seed_data_creation: None. Backup/restore tested read-only (backup downloads + non-zip rejection + empty-zip uploads restore returning count=0). Factory reset tested contract-only (400 without confirm code, 401/403 without auth). Did NOT run actual destructive factory-reset on live data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backup endpoints: GET /api/admin/backup/{database|uploads|info} + POST /api/admin/restore/{database|uploads} + POST /api/admin/factory-reset. Factory reset requires exact {confirm:'DELETE_ALL_DATA'} in body. Admin auth uses POST /api/auth/login returning access_token. /admin/backup page uses data-testid attributes: backup-admin, stat-{documents|collections|files}, download-{db|uploads}-btn, restore-{db|uploads}-{file|btn}, factory-reset-card, show-factory-reset-btn, factory-reset-confirm, factory-reset-btn. Zip-slip vulnerability + temp-file cleanup on restore endpoints noted as non-blocking code review items.
rca of the issue: N/A - all requested features working correctly.
